lines.en_lit: not to begin in front of you, lest you be burned.
The Sages taught “And he shall put the incense upon the fire before the Lord” he not prepare outside, and bring to exclude the opinion of the Sadducees, who say he prepare outside bring
What did interpret; “For I will appear in a cloud upon the Ark cover” teaches that he should prepare outside, he bring
The Sages said to them: Wasn’t it already stated “And he shall put the incense upon the fire before the Lord” If so, what the verse states: “For I will appear in a cloud upon the Ark cover”

teaches that he includes a smoke-raiser in its And from where that he includes a smoke-raiser in its As it is stated: “And the incense cloud shall cover the Ark cover” he did not include a smoke-raiser, or if he omitted any one of its spices, he is liable death
And let derive as his entry for no purpose.
Rav Sheshet said: With what are we dealing here? where he unwittingly entry, and he intentionally with burning,

Rav Ashi said Even you say he intentionally with both this and that, And where he brought two incense one complete and one incomplete, For entry he is not liable as he brought in complete for burning he is liable as he burns incomplete incense.
The Master said And from where that he includes a smoke-raiser in its The verse states: “And it shall cover” verse for verse?
Rav Yosef said: This is said; I have nothing other a leaf of a smoke-raising From where a root of a smoke-raiser? The verse states: “And it shall cover,”

Abaye said But wasn’t the opposite taught As it was taught He put into a root of a smoke-raiser, would rise in a column like a staff until it reached ceiling. When it reached ceiling descended the walls until the House was filled with smoke, as it is stated: “And the House was filled with smoke”
Rather, Abaye said this is said; I have nothing other a root of a smoke-raising From where a leaf of a smoke-raiser? The verse states: “And it shall cover.”
Rav Sheshet said I have nothing other the Tent of Meeting that in the wilderness; from where Shiloh and the eternal Temple The verse states: “And it shall cover,”
This is derived from “And so shall he do for the Tent of Meeting that dwells with them”
Rather, this is said: I have nothing other on Yom Kippur from where on the rest of the days of the year The verse states: “And it shall cover,”
Rav Ashi said: One the mitzva and one indispensable, Rava said: One punishment and one as a warning
It was taught Rabbi Eliezer says: that he not die” punishment “For I will appear in a cloud warning

might both were said before the deaths of Aaron’s sons, the verse states: “After the deaths of the two sons of Aaron” might both of them, were said after the deaths of Aaron’s two sons. the verse states: “For I will appear in a cloud upon the Ark cover How so; warning before death and the punishment after the death
What is the derivation? Rava said the verse states: “For I will appear in a cloud”; had not yet appeared, Rather, what is the reason were punished

as it was taught Rabbi Eliezer says: Aaron’s sons died only because they taught a halakha before Moses their teacher; What did they interpret “And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire upon the altar” although fire descends from the heavens, mitzva to bring man-made

exits and comes the way he entered, From where are these matters Rabbi Shmuel bar Naḥmani said Rabbi Yonatan said the verse states: “And Solomon came to the high place that was at Gibeon, Jerusalem” Now, what does Gibeon have to do with Jerusalem?

Rather, compares his exit from Gibeon to Jerusalem to his arrival from Jerusalem to Gibeon. Just as his arrival from Jerusalem to Gibeon his face was turned toward the high place, in the usual manner arrival, so too, his exit from Gibeon to Jerusalem his face turned toward the high place, in the manner of his arrival.

And likewise, priests in their service; and Levites on their platform and Israelites at their watches, When they departed they would not turn their faces and walk but would turn their faces sideways and walk,

And likewise, a student who takes leave of his teacher not turn his face and walk but turn his face sideways and walk. in accordance with that of Rabbi Elazar when he took leave of his teacher, Rabbi Yoḥanan. When Rabbi Yoḥanan wanted to leave Rabbi Elazar would bend down and stand in his place until Rabbi Yoḥanan disappeared from his sight;

And when Rabbi Elazar wanted to leave, he would walk backward until he disappeared from Rabbi Yoḥanan’s sight, When Rava took leave of Rav Yosef, he would walk backward, until his legs were bruised and the threshold of Rav Yosef’s house was bloodied
